rows.canonical: lexicon alias (per subsystem, qualify the word: lexicon alias / curated concept alias / latent alias / facet alias / graph alias map)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (a): one word, five different concepts in five keyspaces. Only meaning 1 is normalize_identity-keyed and retrieval-live; meaning 3 is captured-but-unread; meanings 2 and 5 are separate curated stores. Any plan sentence saying just 'aliases' is ambiguous.
rows.evidence: grep -rn aliases across backend/services hits corpus_lexicon.py, query_semantics.py:527, summary_semantics.py:100, facets/normalizer.py:438, neo4j_writer.py:43 — five independent producers, no shared type.

rows.canonical: versioned alias registry (to be built, S5)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (b) risk: three curated alias data stores ALREADY exist (CONCEPT_ALIASES in query_semantics.py:527, entity_aliases.json neo4j_writer.py:43, canonical_families.json neo4j_writer.py:46) plus the machine-built corpus_lexicon. If S5 builds the registry as a new store instead of absorbing these, it becomes the 4th/5th parallel alias system — exactly what checklist line 817 forbids ('Keep one corpus-lexicon system; do not create a parallel concept store').
rows.evidence: PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:84 (S5: 'corpus_lexicon/vocabulary join -> versioned alias registry -> gated query-expansion consumption; fold P0.5 curated-aliases-out-of-Python into the same registry'). No code symbol named registry exists (grep alias_registry = 0 hits). Nearest existing seams: corpus_lexicon materialize_entries (corpus_lexicon.py:1593) for storage+epoch invalidation, and the versioned-data pattern of shelf_policy_data.py:3 ('DATA, not logic: owner-curated, versioned as a unit').

code_symbols.role: THE identity normalizer: NFKC, lower, _/- -> space, strip non-alnum, collapse spaces
query-side exact-term generation (query_exact_terms, vocabulary.py:65)
card/shelf key derivation

rows.collision_note: DISTINCT from the graph normalizer normalize_entity_name (neo4j_writer.py:812: NFKD, keeps underscores because \w matches _, deletes hyphens WITHOUT inserting a space). Example divergence: 'Box2D-Lite' -> normalize_identity 'box2d lite' vs normalize_entity_name 'box2dlite'. Two legitimate keyspaces, but code that mixes them silently breaks joins (see entity_id row).
rows.evidence: corpus_lexicon.py:192-198 vs neo4j_writer.py:812-817 — different unicode form, different hyphen/underscore treatment.

S5 should feed registry aliases through this existing gate, not build a second expansion path.
rows.evidence: checklist:54 'Query expansion cannot replace or semantically drift from the original'; query_plan.py:5 'keeps the original user query as the mandatory recall lane'.

store_fields: Neo4j Entity.entity_id (indexed; canonical per memory + fact-seed fix)

rows.canonical: entity_id (neo4j_writer.entity_id_from_name is the single source of truth)
rows.collision_note: FAILURE MODE (b) CONFIRMED IN CODE: two id builders exist. They agree on plain multi-word names but DIVERGE on hyphen/underscore/unicode names: 'Box2D-Lite' -> graph 'entity:box2dlite' vs lexicon fallback 'entity:box2d-lite'; and the lexicon fallback skips the curated entity_aliases.json merge entirely. Any lexicon entity_ids minted by the :567 fallback can fail the vocabulary.py:851 Neo4j join silently — same class of bug as the underscore-slug incident fixed in b40da18.
rows.evidence: neo4j_writer.py:1112-1127 vs corpus_lexicon.py:567-569; normalizer divergence shown at neo4j_writer.py:812-817 vs corpus_lexicon.py:192-198.
